page: Amazon Web Services r5b.2xlarge is a memory-optimized fifth-generation server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The configuration includes 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across multi-core processing and memory operations. The instance achieves average ratings in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and Passmark memory scores. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput, reaching 376.63 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 44.25 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core, gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% of tested hardware.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local storage or high-performance single-core execution. Factual benchmark data shows average performance in database operations, Redis SET operations at 6.89 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 7.1 GB/sec. Consequently, it is suited for relational databases, caching layers, and text processing tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides a dense memory profile for applications requiring large working sets without the added cost of local NVMe storage or specialized GPU accelerators.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. It provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though single-core execution speeds represent a performance tradeoff, ranking in the bottom 25%. Benchmark results show average throughput for Redis SET operations at 6.89 million operations per second and static web serving at 7.1 GB/sec. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and text processing where high memory density is required but local storage is not.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering average multi-core and database performance for memory-intensive workloads.
